## Description

Applies Anthropic official brand colors and typography to artifacts. Use when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.

## When to Use

Use this skill when:

- Creating branded artifacts
- Applying company colors
- Using official typography
- Following design standards
- Building branded presentations
- Creating branded documents
- Designing branded web pages

**Triggers**: "Apply brand colors", "Use company branding", "Follow brand guidelines", "Make it look like Anthropic", "Apply official colors"

## When NOT to Use

- For non-Anthropic projects
- For generic design (use frontend-design instead)
- For custom brand systems (use theme-factory instead)
- For code styling (use code-docs instead)

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Understand Artifact

- Identify artifact type
- Review current design
- Note color/typography needs
- Plan brand application

### Phase 2: Apply Branding

- Apply official colors
- Use official typography
- Follow spacing guidelines
- Maintain consistency

### Phase 3: Verify

- Check color contrast
- Verify typography
- Test on different devices
- Get feedback

### Phase 4: Finalize

- Make adjustments
- Export final version
- Document decisions
- Archive for reuse

## Tools & References

**Related Skills**:

- frontend-design - Design UI
- canvas-design - Create visual art
- theme-factory - Create themes
- marp-slide - Create presentations

**Brand Assets**:

- Official colors
- Typography guidelines
- Logo usage
- Spacing standards

## Best Practices

- **Consistency**: Use official colors everywhere
- **Accessibility**: Ensure sufficient contrast
- **Typography**: Use official fonts
- **Spacing**: Follow spacing guidelines
- **Documentation**: Document brand decisions

## Skills Required

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `frontend-design` | Design UI with brand alignment |
| `canvas-design` | Create visual art with brand colors |
| `theme-factory` | Apply consistent themes across artifacts |

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Artifact type identified and brand requirements confirmed
- [ ] Official colors applied consistently
- [ ] Typography follows brand guidelines
- [ ] Color contrast meets accessibility standards
- [ ] Spacing follows brand standards
- [ ] Brand decisions documented

## Pitfalls

- Using non-official colors breaks brand consistency
- Insufficient color contrast fails accessibility checks
- Mixing multiple brand systems in one artifact confuses viewers
- Not documenting brand decisions makes future updates harder
